channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Iranian Missile Sites Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States announced it conducted airstrikes on Iranian missile sites positioned near the Strait of Hormuz, a vital chokepoint for global oil shipments. President Donald Trump confirmed the ope...

item.title: Woolworths Acquires in2food to Strengthen Supply Chain and Expand Internationally
item.description: Woolworths has officially acquired its long-standing food supplier, in2food, marking a strategic move to enhance both local and international operations. in2food, a privately-owned prepared foods manu...

item.title: Helio Castroneves to Compete for Historic Fifth Indy 500 Victory
item.description: Brazilian racing veteran Helio Castroneves is set to make history by attempting to become the first driver to win the Indianapolis 500 five times. Castroneves, who will turn 51 just two weeks before t...

item.title: Iran Rejects Peace Efforts Following Israel's Targeted Killing of Security Chief
item.description: On March 17, 2026, Israel confirmed the targeted killing of Iran's senior security official, Ali Larijani, along with his son and deputy, marking the highest-profile assassination since the start of t...

item.title: Teaser Released for Denis Villeneuve’s Dune Part Three Featuring Timothée Chalamet
item.description: The teaser for Denis Villeneuve’s much-awaited film 'Dune: Part Three' has been released, generating excitement among fans of the sci-fi saga. The movie, set to release on 18th December, features a st...

item.title: A Father’s Documentary Journey Through Love, Loss and Grief
item.description: Norwegian director Gunnar Hall Jensen presents Portrait of a Confused Father, a deeply personal documentary chronicling his son Jonathan's life from birth in 2002 until his untimely death in 2023. The...

item.title: Zanzibar Records Slight Increase in Annual Inflation to 4.82% in February
item.description: In Zanzibar, annual inflation went up to 4.82 percent in February 2026 from 4.25 percent in January, according to the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) report from the Office of the Chief Government S...

item.title: Lahore Court Extends Bail for TikTokers in Online Gambling Case
item.description: LAHORE: On Tuesday, a sessions court in Lahore extended the interim pre-arrest bail of popular TikTok creators Rajab Butt and Nadeem Mubarak, also known as Naniwala, who are facing allegations of prom...

item.title: Meningitis outbreak in Kent: 15 cases confirmed and infant in critical condition
item.description: A serious outbreak of meningococcal meningitis has been declared a 'national incident' in Kent, with 15 confirmed cases and two deaths. Among those critically affected is nine-month-old Nala-Rose Flet...

item.title: House committee reviews Sara Duterte impeachment complaints after her formal response
item.description: The House of Representatives’ Committee on Justice resumed its deliberations on the impeachment complaints filed against Vice President Sara Duterte on Wednesday, March 18, 2026. This marks the fourth...

item.title: SA Agriculture Minister Highlights Border Challenges in Controlling Foot and Mouth Disease
item.description: South Africa's Agriculture Minister, John Steenhuisen, has identified the lack of a proper border fence between South Africa and Zimbabwe, along with damaged redline fences, as major factors contribut...

item.title: ODM Nairobi Delegates Endorse Winnie Odinga for Deputy Party Leadership to Boost Regional Representation
item.description: The Nairobi delegates of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) have officially endorsed Winnie Odinga for the position of deputy party leader, citing the need for proper representation of the capital i...

item.title: Benue Monarch Appeals for Government Support After Heavy Rain Causes Flooding
item.description: People of Otukpo in Benue State don dey suffer after heavy rain wash through town, damage plenty houses and make business waka slow down. His Royal Majesty Agabaidu Elaigwu Odogbo Obagaji John, the Oc...

item.title: A Complete Guide to Adopting Miniature and Toy Poodles
item.description: Adopting a Miniature or Toy Poodle can be a deeply rewarding experience, but it requires careful preparation and understanding of the breed. These small poodles are intelligent, active, and people-ori...

item.title: Kenyan and Chinese Man Face Charges for Illegal Dealing in Live Ants
item.description: Nairobi — The Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) has formally charged a 37-year-old Chinese national, Zhang Kequn, together with a 35-year-old Kenyan, Charles Mwangi, for illegal trade in live wild...

item.title: UN Reports Over 36,000 Palestinians Displaced in Occupied West Bank in One Year Due to Settlement Expansion
item.description: According to a fresh report from the United Nations human rights office, more than 36,000 Palestinians were displaced in the occupied West Bank within the past year, highlighting a dramatic escalation...

item.title: TISEZA to Take Over Management of Buzwagi Special Economic Zone to Boost Investment
item.description: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Governance, Constitutional and Legal Affairs in Tanzania has recommended that TISEZA (Tanzania Investment and Special Economic Zones Authority) takes over manag...

item.title: CERN's LHCb Detects New Doubly Charmed Baryon Xi-cc-plus After Detector Upgrade
item.description: The Large Hadron Collider (LHC) at CERN has identified a new subatomic particle called Xi-cc-plus, marking the 80th particle discovered by this powerful machine. Announced on Tuesday, this baryon cons...

item.title: Howard secures historic first NCAA tournament win over UMBC
item.description: Howard University achieved a historic milestone on Tuesday night, defeating UMBC 86-83 in the First Four round of the NCAA tournament. Bryce Harris led the Bison with 19 points and 14 rebounds, includ...

item.title: NIOB Blames Weak Laws and Poor Enforcement for Frequent Building Collapses in Nigeria
item.description: The Nigerian Institute of Building (NIOB) don yan say the plenty cases of building collapse for Nigeria na because of weak construction laws and failure to punish people wey dey responsible. Daniel Ko...
